commit.message: Stop messages for applying bog out of LoS

Make volcanoes roar less

Makes use of the currently unused countdown_limit parameter. Also fixed
an unpaired parenthesis.

Fixes #3858.

Fix a crash with dynamic map markers

This was typically happening in the Abyss, when the map shifted with
Malign Gateway active; the game crashed when it timed out.

This happened because map_marker::move was creating a duplicate marker
in dynamic_markers, which later caused problems in run_all.

Fixes 5280
Fix wizmode transforming into the null form

Forbid training magic skills under Trog

Some details:
* Unlike Ru-sacced skills, you keep your existing levels
* These skills are hidden from the skills menu (even if trained), but
showable on "all" in case you want to abandon.
* Gnolls continue to get an exemption from the conduct.

Also merge Trog's no-memorising and no-casting forbiddances

Don't show gaps in the show-all skill menu

Skills we don't show even in "show all" (e.g. weapons skills for felids)
were still getting their own line.

Remove some state from skill management

Previously, when we changed the player's equipment, spells, etc, we
registered our intent to show or hide a skill from the ones shown in the
skill menu, and this intent was then applied at some future time.

Instead, we just calculate which skills to show as needed (which is only
in the skill menus). Actually, we already have all the functionality to
do this, since the state we are removing wasn't cached on save.

This also removes an explicit check for skill on our active talisman -
it's a non-useless item in our inventory and is checked as such.

Remove a redundant array

can_currently_train[sk] was always equal to !is_useless_skill(sk), which
is rather easier to reason about (and is still very cheap to call).

Allow scrolls of amnesia under Trog

If anything Trog is probably in favour of you forgetting your forbidden
magic.

List spells even when none are memorisable

Now god-hated spells are not memorisable, it is important to let the
player view their library (e.g. when worshipping Trog, when it is all
useless). And this is also quite a bit clearer than useless spells just
disappearing.

Useless are listed last, so don't get in the way of comprehension.

Describe uselessness on more items

These are useful to explain the new god-forbidden items mechanic, and
also seems generally worth having.

Tweak logic for god-approved acquirement items

This is mostly a refactor to remove an unnecessary second category of
god-based hatred ("despising")

Refactor many negative conducts into forbidden acts

Following the previoius sequence of commits, most things gods dislike
are now entirely forbidden rather than merely disapproved of. We
refactor so that this split is clear and reflected in god descriptions.

Some gods still dislike targetting certain neutral or allied creatures;
this is rather harder to change because of the number of ways there are
to hurt something.

While doing this, we also reorganise Zin's conducts/forbiddances a
little. "Unclean" was redundant with "Evil+Chaotic", and is removed.
Some bits of "chaotic" (talismans, polymorph etc) made sense to sit
in the same forbiddance as "mutation", which we now call
"transformation". None of this changes the set of things that Zin
forbids.

Remove various now-unreachable penance checks

After a sequence of commits migrating penance to forbidding
items/spells, there is now no way to trigger these (and if there is, we
should fix that with forbidding the activity or item that causes it)

Forbid animating weapons hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

For visible monsters, you are stopped before casting.
For invisible ones, you waste a turn.

Forbid throwing items hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

Forbid hated spells rather than applying penance

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

Forbid evoking items hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

We also start showing useless evokers in the evoke menu, as otherwise
it might be quite confusing when they disappear for god-related reasons.

Forbid scrolls and potions hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

The most interesting choice here is potions of mutation under Zin.
While locking in bad mutation sets until Zin's cleansing comes online
might sometimes be quite bad, removing the exception simplifies things a
good deal both in terms of player understanding and the code, and other
gods have rather harsher conducts (especially now the worst of the bad
mutations have been removed or softened).

Forbid equipment hated by your god.

This updates from gods causing penance for hated items to outright
forbidding them.

Gods will now reject your worship until you remove forbidden items and
forms. If rolled from ecumenical altars, they will forcibly remove them
from you (bypassing your form to remove melded items).

This is the first in a series of commits that will move many instances
of penance to outright bans. There are a few reasons for this:
- The rare cases that accepting penance or passively wielding a banned
item are correct are rather unflavourful.
- Penance and piety loss are confusing, and the distinction is poorly
explained (many warnings about this lie to the player).
- Codewise the current situation is quite fragile; warnings and actual
penance have to be kept in sync in many places.
Make god_hates_monster a boolean

Show useless armour in wear menu

This is consistent with weapons and jewellery, which show e.g. objects
that the species can never wear, and it can be confusing not to find
things you know you have.

Fix a few artefact properties to message on meld/unmeld

These match the mundane versions which are more accurate

Fix displaying messages when destroying melded equipment

When melded equipment was destroyed (via mutations) we were triggering
the normal "unequip" code. This printed out a bunch of harmless but
incorrect messages about the properties of the equipment disappearing.

Prevent good mutations from occasionally removing evolution/rMut

These were marked as an "inverse pair", which meant that when one
was rolled (as either a good or random mutation) it could remove
the other. This was especially visible and bad-feeling when the
player was rolling a guaranteed good mutation.

Fix an occasional misreport from mutation()

In certain conflicts with innate mutations, the mutation() function was
doing nothing but saying it succeeded, which led to some consequences
like Jiyva thinking they've given you a mutation or your evolution
counter ticking.

This does not affect the actual mutations given by the function, as we
were blocking the deletion of the innate mutation later.
Fix a build warning by making something const
Don't highlight ancestors in discord targeter (Emily Bunny)

Add rudimentary Makefile support for profiling

This commit adds a PROFILING make argument that, when defined, adds the
-pg argument for compilation and linking. This can can be used to
generate a gmon.out file suitable for analysis in gprof. I'm not going
to add any further support or documentation to this end, since I'm not
yet sure what further capabilities will be needed.

Add a dancing-related quote for Uskayaw altars

[skip ci]

Add an arachnine quote

[skip ci]

Add some quotes about insects

[skip ci]

Specify UTF-8 encoding in python generation utilities

With recent fancy glyph characters, some CI systems and servers are
failing to run these scripts. We fix this by specifying the file
encoding as UTF-8 (slightly speculatively, as I don't have access to an
equivalent system to build on), and remove the more local fix from
0a0f4f6.

Also remove some misleading messages about scripts working with python 2
(they almost certainly didn't before or after this change).

Fix a very small error in displayed to-hit chances

Maximum impact was ~1% for normal attacks and ~2% for auxes.

Fix a very small error in displayed hex success chances

The maximum impact was 1%, so similar to rounding errors in the display.

Don't crash when aiming beams at monsters with 0 EV

(Often because they're asleep.)

Fix to-hit estimate for player beam spells against monsters

The function worked by computing all possible combinations of to-hit and
evasion rolls for a given spell versus a given monster, then returning what
percentage of those combinations resulted in a hit.

However, those combinations are *not equally likely* since the range of the
second and third EV roll are directly dependent on the result of the first
roll.

This caused the UI to underestimate the player's hit chance - sometimes
very substantially. In a worst case, the game could report a ~9% chance to
hit The Enchantress when your actual hit chance was ~30% (against less
evasive targets, it might report ~85% as ~75%, which is less dramatic, but
still impactful.)

We now calculate the odds of each *initial* roll resulting in a hit, and
then average those chances (since each result of the initial roll *is*
equally likely).
Another attempt at fixing MSVC CI issues
Limit path macros to dependent objects

Potentially fix a CI issue with the MSCV build

Though I admit I'm somewhat guessing here.

Allow positive mutations while at innate stat-zero (AliasTheSpectator)

We were vetoing all good mutations if the character was at stat zero
ignoring gear; fix this so we only veto ones which reduce a stat which
is then below zero (still ignoring gear).

Make screaming refractions nonliving horrors

Their undeadness wasn't obvious (in my opinion, anyway), and undeath is not
needed to explain how someone's physical being could end up deeply muddled
due to the power of the Abyss.

Allow using the mon_glyph to remap Bai Suzhen's dragon form and SoH

The serpents of hell all shared the same in-game name, making mon_glyph
only able to remap the first of them. The same was true of Bai Suzhen's
dragon form.

Now you can do, eg:
mon_glyph += serpent of hell (coc) : ice D
if one wants.

Fix some living spells not being named properly

(Presumably reflecting an early state of development, but not even one
which ever reached trunk, I believe.)

Recolour living spells based on their spell in console

Now that there is plenty of room on the glyph.

(And change the colour of some walking tomes to better match their spell.)

Recolour derived undead based on HD instead of size

The color split was historically for small/large zombie, but size is only
loosely correlated with threat level (whereas that spriggan defender zombie
among a bunch of larger chaff is pretty noteworthy). The additional damage
from simulacra and draugr is heavily HD-scaled, after all.

The cutoff of 10 HD is fairly arbitrary, but reasonable-seeming to me.

Make merged slime creatures use a gradiant of colors in console

Instead of single subtle change differentiating '1' from all other sizes of
slime creatures, take advantage of not having many other monsters on the
same glyph now to use 4 colours instead:
 green->lightblue->lightcyan->lightmagenta

Change glyphs used for expiring clouds in console

While the progression used had a somewhat pleasing 'fading circle' look to
them, in some standard console fonts, ○ and o were nearly
indistinguishable (which has been the source of at least some player
complaints). A few of the new unicode monster glyphs are also uncomfortably
similar, so I'm trying out a replacement.

I've attempted to use characters more unlike any monster, and aimed for
something that vaguely looks like the full-duration cloud glyph (§) slowly
unravelling over time (whereas none of the ones in the previous schema
looked very much like §). Hopefully this doesn't feel unduely disruptive,
considering.

Split and rearrange many monster glyphs to make use of unicode

This commit aims to reduce both current and future overlap among many
glyphs that were already overly full (or nearly so), accounting especially
for monsters likely to be added as part of forthcoming extended reworks. I
also hope to improve clarity in a few cases where many different things
were packed into one glyph (for instance, * folded together
pseudo-firewood, projectiles, stationary things that explode, and also some
of the individually strongest monsters in the game, for a total of 24
different monsters on one glyph).

The major category changes are as follows

'h' is once more only for canines.
 -Most of its other residents are moved to 'a', which is now 'misc animal'

'9' has been split into 3:
 -'ŋ' for sapient beings (eg: gargoyles, nargun)
 -'9' for lesser golems
 -'8' for greater golems

Elves now get both 'e'/'E' (for low/high-tier elves)

Elementals have been moved to 'Σ'

Unique giants are now on 'Č'

Unique dragons are now on 'Ď'

Seraphs are now 'Å' (in anticipation of Purgatory adding 2 more Seraphs)

Jellies now get both 'j'/'J' (for low/high-tier jellies)
 -Gnolls are moved to 'ğ'

'z' is now purely for skeletons
 -Wights are moved to 'U', while death cobs are moved to 'f'
 -Floating skulls have been moved from 'z' to 'ž'

'U' is now for 'non-derived corporeal undead', absorbing all of what was
previously on 'n', plus a few more things.

'n' is now for 'nature spirit', taking dryads and eleionoma from 'R' and
water nymphs from 'm'

'R' is now more clearly for 'demonic spirit'
 -Ancestors are moved to their own glyph, 'Æ', and now each have different
  colors

'Q' is expanded to 'bird-like person', absorbing harpies and chonchon

'H' has been split into 'H'/'Þ' for non-flying, flying hybrid monsters
 (eg: minotaurs are still 'H', while sphinxes are 'Þ')

'λ' is now primarily for moths, leaving other winged insects on 'y'

'◊' is now for living/plant firewood, taking many things out of 'P' and
 a couple '*'
 -P should now contain only active plants

'I' has been split into (at least) 3:
 -'I' for proper statue monsters (eg: ice statue)
 -'‡' for other nonliving stationary monsters, such as turrets or sawblades
 -'Δ' for nonliving firewood (eg: stack of scrap, splinterfrost barricade)
  (This used to be the 'detected item' glyph, which I changed to ¿)
 -Tesseracts get their own glyph: '╬'
 -(A few instances of living immobile firewood were moved into '◊')

Ghost monsters have been reorganized:
 -'ŵ' for 'support' ghosts or pseudo-projectiles (eg: silent spectre,
  drowned souls) who lack other direct attacks (including lost souls)
 -'W' for low/mid-tier ghosts with attacks (including soul wisps)
 -'Ŵ' for high-tier ghosts (eg: flayed ghost, eidola)
 -Player ghosts have been moved to 'Z' (as a form of derived undead)
 -(Poltergeists remain on 'v')

'ω' is for tentacle (tips)

Orc apostles are now on 'ö' and are coloured according to their class

'ψ' is for 'evil person', pulling many evil mages and priests out of 'p'
 -Ragged hierophants and imperial myrmidons join them, while servants of
  whispers move to 'p'

'*' has been heavily subdivided:
 -'*' is for things that explode (eg: ball lightning, prisms)
 -'●' is for non-explosive projectiles (eg: foxfire and orb of destruction)
 -'Θ' is for actual proper monsters (eg: orbs of fire)
 -'¤' is for 'none of the above' or possibly 'floating support thing'
  (eg: battlespheres)
 -(Certain pseudo-firewood is also moved to '◊'/'Δ')

If the 'char_set = ascii' option is used, each of these Unicode glyphs will
be replaced by the nearest thematic match among ASCII glyphs. This is
somewhat imperfect in a few cases (eg: most ◊ used to be P, but some used
to be I), but maintaining a separate fallback glyph and color for every
single monster added in perpetuity seems overly burdensome given how rarely
the option is used. mon_glyph can still be used to adjust any of this to a
given player's taste.

(I realize there are quite a few outdated comments throughout the codebase
referring to old glyph categories, but some of these were already wrong for
a decade, and I'm leaving any fixups there until this commit settles, at
the very least.)

Slightly improve monster::foot_name() and hand_name()

Reduce dependence on monster glyphs (since some of those will be moving
shortly) and fix a numbers of oversights due to parts of this not being
updated since 2008 (sheep and apises and Asterion didn't have hooves,
bog bodies had claws but revenants did not - among other things).

This is still definitely wrong and incomplete, and wants a more thorough
refactoring to some form that is less guaranteed to constantly fall out of
date with new monsters (perhaps some expansion of monster shapes?), but
it's also used almost entirely for jinxbite messages and monster miscasts
(which are themselves on the chopping block), so it doesn't seem worth the
time that would be required right now.

Allow mon_glyph option to accept elemental colors

It stopped being able to in 9c2c487 but it's not clear to me why this would
have ever been a problematic case (after all, monster glyphs are the main
place we use elemental colors in the first place), and it seems to work
without issue once re-enabled.

Allow specifying monster base glyphs in unicode

While Crawl has used unicode characters for its features for many, many
years now, and also allowed remapping monsters to arbitrary unicode glyphs
via user settings, the default glyphs for all monsters were restricted to
ASCII.

But several monster glyphs have been overfull for years and the forthcoming
extended rework will place further pressure on them. In order to free up
more room, this allows monster base glyphs to be specified in unicode. A
subsequent commit will reorganize many monsters to make use of this.

Fix undeadness of undead species.

Oops.
Make formatting of form YAML fields more consistent

Prevent evasion of divine bans on forms

It was previously possibly to worship gods in forms they banned, by
using flux baubles to join them. This for example allowed players to be
liches worshipping the shining one (happily using its various evil
abilities with no repurcussions).

Also correctly check for vampire form in the pre-transform form check;
good gods already checked for this via another code path, but this
improves the message slightly. While doing this, it seemed cleaner to
make the undeadness of a form a property of that form.
Add missing includes

Remove forest vines on tree death and fix tile bugs.

Summon forest vines, like all tentacles, pick their tile based on their
parent and child tentacles. For vines, the parent can be a tree.

Before this commit, the first vine in the chain finds its parent by
looking for an adjacent tree. This has interesting consequences
when there isn't such a tree, which can happen in two ways:
1) If the tree is destroyed, or
2) If the spell has ended (but the tentacle is out of LoS so still
assumed to be there).

When this happens, the code cannot find a parent, and so assumes that
the vine is half-submerged, like kraken tentacles used to be sometimes.
However, because vines don't _have_ half-submerged tiles, this ends up
going out of the range of vine tiles and selecting a starspawn tile.

We fix this by:
- Making vines remember which tree they are anchored to. This lets us
display the correct tile for out-of-LoS vines even when the supporting
tree has disappeared.
- When a tree is destroyed, we re-anchor vines where possible, and
destroy them if there is no alternative tree.

Update screen between deck of destruction cards

When dealing 4 from Nemelex's deck, the screen was not updated between
cards, which made it hard to make decisions for the next cards.

Similarly, torment and acid waves were not updating before the bolt
phase of the card.

Remove poison when transforming into an undead/non-living form.

While intentional and reasonable flavourwise, poison going inert when
in an unliving form is consistently surprising to players and the
indefinite status is clutter.

Another option discussed was to make poison damage stop but the duration
still tick down (like other sources of poison immunity). However, it
seems more realistic for liches etc to never be poisoned at all.

From a gameplay perspective, this should hardly matter at all - the
player being able to safely transform to one of these forms but not
able to transform back when safe is likely to be extremely rare.

Fixes #5176, #5218
break instead of return

Fix several duel bugs

Skip some on floor entry effects when the player is returning from the arena.
This prevents retriggering hell effects, gulch mutations, slime stair regen,
and resetting abyss timer.

Fixes #5276.

Clear entirely known parchments/books off our shopping list

We already had a very fine method for doing this, but sadly it hasn't
been called for six years (since b274e46). This was masked in many cases
by the culling of identical items.

Fixes #5281
Give a message when attempting to swap with -move

Describe temporary trees as flammable

Trees from summon forest have been able to burn since 7cfa934; fix
their description to match.
Fix checkwhite

Don't falsely warn for barbs on crocodile movement

To do this we generalise check_moveto, which was taking a "physcially"
argument but using it for two somewhat different purposes.

Make fusillade hurt wall walkers

Both before and after this change, fusillade can be centred on wall
monsters, but before this it would not damage them either with these
or adjacent explosions.

Fix a bug with exclusions for new enemies on descent

When we traverse stairs and see a new monster which should be
autoexcluded, we were previously placing an exclusion without the
monster name in its description, and then immediately removed it before
the player could even see it.

This was happening because the autoexclusions happen before our
map_knowledge is updated. We switch the order of these updates so that
autoexclusions are calculated with the updated knowledge.

Fix Eringya's Surprising Crocodile movement effects

When the spell moved the player 1 tile and created shallow water there,
movement effects like barbs and icy armour breaking weren't triggering.

This happened because the terrain change was calling
trigger_movement_effects, clearing the deferred move.

This does mean that if there were any movement effects that should be
suppressed by the terrain change, we'd trigger them anyway. But since
we only change terrain if it is plain floor, this shouldn't be a
problem in practice.

Fix death channel creating ghosts after the effect expires.

This could happen when killing monsters with various end-of-turn effect
such as yak form walls and fusillade.

The sequence of events is:
- Player turn happens; monsters killed are marked for ghosting.
- Monsters killed on turn produce ghosts.
- End of turn sequence; monsters killed are marked for ghosting.
- Death channel expires, all existing ghosts disappear.
- Monsters killed by end of turn sequence produce ghosts.
- <These ghosts persist until death channel expires again>

We fix this by checking death channel is still up when producing
ghosts.

Closes #3040
Autoexclude monsters seen using revelation (#5261)

commit.message: Fix a link in the new dev guide

New electricity animation tile effects

A new suite of effect tiles heavily modified from an old rejected
electrocution brand effect by Bloax, repurposed and remixed for nearly
every other electric effect in the game instead (with one of Denzi's
generic blasts), nearly all replacing the same generic turqoise blasts
of Denzi's. There are four categories of effects deployed here matched
up loosely according to their mechanics:

 * Weaker projectiles:
   * Electrical Bolt, Stunning Burst, Blinkbolt, weaker shock serpent
     discharges.
 * Stronger projectiles:
   * Unleash Destruction, Lightning Bolt, Call Down Lightning, Plasma
     Beam, Xom's lightning (additional shots), stronger shock serpent
     discharges.
 * Electric explosions
   * Conjure Ball Lightning, Orb of Electrocution, Fulsome Fusillade
     rolling electric explosions, lightning rod Thunderbolt, Nemelex's
     Storm card, Xom's lightning (initial blast).
 * Electric arcs and chains
   * Static Discharge, Arcjolt, Galvanic Breath, Eel Form slaps
     and eeljolts.

This also updates Makhleb's Unleash Destruction effect for magma and ice
effects, and makes shock serpent discharges have a brief visual effect on
those injured by them.

(Shock does not change its current graphic, which is understated enough
to reasonably match its damage levels. Chain Lightning also does not join
the rest of these effects due to its partially-resistable nature wanting
for a fairly visually distinct effect- it using the generic cyan blast is
now actually fairly unique for the spell school, ironically enough, and
can function for now.)

Quick micro vault review

Some draugr vaults further mildly nerfed, another teleport island getting
hatches, making minmay_crystal_box have a one-time escape and more
revealed when approaching it in late D, and dealing with some math isuees
in a Gulch entry. Most notably, this also removes a typo'd "splevels"
from Trove randbooks, which was not preserved since slevels caps book
spell levels rather than providing any target to reach.

Remove unique annotations on thralling (Undo)

Uniques' names change from X to Vampire X when they are enthralled -
move the annotation removal before this so that it matches the name
correctly

Fix !w inscriptions for equipping and unequipping

The 1 and i characters look too much alike.

Fixes #5263

Add Odds to the Dev Team and update docs

Welcome aboard, Odds! 🥳

Fix a crash with untransforming into lich form

Transforming out of lich form with eg. a flux bauble, casting death's door,
then ending transformation to return to lich form crashed the game because
the untransform wouldn't complete successfully. Additionally, death's door
could be maintained in vampire form (which forbids casting it) using this
same method. For consistency and to fix the crash, entering either undead
form while in death's door now ends the death's door status. This will
prompt for normal transformation; probably a prompt should be added for
untransform but I was unsure where to put it.

Closes #5277

Fix some false freezing cloud warnings. (#5264)

Noticed that even when worshipping Qazlal, freezing cloud still warns
when casting it on yourself.
Here the problem is two-fold:
* Check on cloud immunity was missing from lambda, could be useful for
monsters too
* Lambda was never called for player.

It seems like `include_player` is true only for freezing cloud check so
it should not affect anything else.

Fix some false freezing cloud warnings. (#5264)

Noticed that even when worshipping Qazlal, freezing cloud still warns
when casting it on yourself.
Here the problem is two-fold:
* Check on cloud immunity was missing from lambda, could be useful for
monsters too
* Lambda was never called for player.

It seems like `include_player` is true only for freezing cloud check so
it should not affect anything else.

commit.message:
Allow PoG on decorative terrain. (#5248)

Make use of the new feat_is_floor function and extend it to runelights.
Closes #5120.

Allow PoG on decorative terrain. (#5248)

Make use of the new feat_is_floor function and extend it to runelights.
Closes #5120.

commit.message:
Fix a startup crash with force_ability_targeter

Attempting to get the name of this ability without having previously used
Exegesis tried to pull up the name of SPELL_NO_SPELL, which asserts (and
this option seemingly results in trying to get the name of all abilities
at startup)

I'm not fully certain if spell_title(SPELL_NO_SPELL) should assert rather
than just return something like "No Spell", but for now I'm fixing this at
the level of Repeat Exegesis itself.

Allow using Imprint Weapon / Imbue Servitor if spell is usable via Exegesis

These two abilities are normally only usable if the player has memorized
Platinum Paragon / Spellspark Servitor. But they are good Exegesis targets,
which created the awkward situation where in order to cast Paragon via
Exegesis at all, you had to first memorize it (with potentially 100%
failure rate), imprint *something* on it, and then likely forget the spell
again.

Instead, make these abilities available to the player if the spell is in
their library and Exegesis is usable. (I had worried this might be slightly
confusing to the player, but it's plausibly less confusing than the way
things currently work).

Fix elementalist ancestors casting LRD on the player for real damage (Lici)

Damage done to the focal point of LRD (ie: if casting it directly on a
creature) wasn't handled via beam code and thus bypassed the protection the
player gets from their own ancestor. However, the tracer also isn't aware
of this, and thinks the player cannot be harmed.

As a result, ancestors would freely deconstruct susceptible players in
order to hit enemies.

It feels a little incoherant to allow them to blow you up and it *not* do
any damage to you, so now all LRD casters are entirely prevented from
directly targeting a creature that is immune to all their effects.

Fix backspace sometimes not working on macOS (perryprog)

Some terminals send keycode 127 (ASCII DEL) when you press the backspace
key. We have special handling for this but were accidentally checking for
keycode -127

Fix a crash-on-demand in some webtiles versions, remove a menu param

When isspace() was called in prompt_invent_item() on non-character keycodes
(such as numpad arrows), it could result in a crash. The function
apparently has undefined behavior for negative numbers which may be
locale-specific (it results in a crash on certain servers and not others,
for instance).

I am not certain what the 'correct' solution is to calling this function on
this sort of keyboard input. Fortunately, it looks like the code in
question is essentially superfluous: spaces already exit almost all menus
at an earlier point in this codepath. The only menus it *doesn't* are
god weapon blessings and Ash cursing/uncursing items. And even in those
cases, it seems to essentially only result in not printing 'Huh?' when you
press space (after already having pressing a letter of an invalid item),
unlike any other invalid input.

I can see no reason these menus need to behave in this narrowly different
way and am simply removing this behavior.

This fixes #5273.

Antimagic(Draining) gaze was only healing ghost moth and this special
case was removed in 0.31. See d43dddf.

commit.message: fix another docs typo

Greatly increase exploration piety gained in Ziggurats

While it is fine (perhaps even desirable) if exploration and kill piety
have slightly different dynamics between various parts of the game, they're
broadly comparable for a majority of it. Zigs, however, have extreme enemy
density compared to the rest of the game. While there's some variance
involved, a mid-speed exploration god earns something like 1/5th the piety
of a standard kill god on their first Zig. Even if this makes some measure
of sense (there's lots of things to kill and not much to explore), I feel
it makes certain gods needlessly poor compared to other gods.

In the interest of more equality, this commit greatly boosts exploration
piety in Zigs. Since Zig maps actually can vary a great deal in size,
depending on which generator a Zig chooses to use, this is also normalized
by the same function Zig generators use to determine map area. The end
result should (very approximately) triple the current piety these gods
receive in Zigs.

Kill gods will still have superior piety income, even in a first zig (and
the gap will widen considerably when megazigging), but this should narrow
the gap enough to make a wider variety of gods able to keep up with Zig
threats while spending their piety actively.

Fix battlespheres sticking to melee range of enemies (qoalabear)

Battlesphere technically don't have spells, which made their threat_range
appear to be 0 since 7ec163d resulting in their M_MAINTAIN_RANGE behavior
no longer working.

Experimentally adjust Knight ancestors

These changes are motivated by several factors:
 -Knights are the plainest design of the 3 ancestor types and have the
  least exciting capstone upgrade (gaining ~22% increased melee damage on
  the ancestor who had by far the lowest melee damage until battlemage was
  replaced)
 -An attempt to lean futher into emphasising their role as a 'tank' by
  offering unique utility to assist with that role (in the same way that
  elementalists lean further away from generalists than battlemages did).
 -(With elementalists no longer gaining haste, certain other devs felt it
  was more odd for 2 of 3 to have it instead of each spell being unique to
  a given ancestor)

Knight ancestors no longer gain Haste at XL 19, but instead gain Bolster
(a similar self-buff that instead grants Might+Resistance). This gives a
little more defensive power in lategame, while providing a similar
offensive boost as before (and leaving the true speed increase to Hexers).

More uniquely, at XL 15 they gain the ability to briefly Bind adjacent
enemies in place with their melee attacks. This is a very short-duration
effect, but can be applied with a high chance - effectively allowing a
knight to 'obstruct' a wider space and catch enemies trying to slip past
them towards a player who might be hiding behind them.

Since it is dependant on their attack speed (and knights attack slowly), it
is less reliable when first gained and improves meaningfully when they gain
speed brand at XL 24 (improving the impact of that upgrade).

(Incidentally, this ability was originally designed to *be* a new XL 24
capstone ability, but playing with it, I felt that it created enough
interesting situations that it merited being tried as a core part of the
knight's kit from much earlier.)

Finally, knights also gain a 25% HP increase at XL 24 to feel like their
final upgrade has a bit more parity with the other two ancestors. (Also:
ancestor durability suffers somewhat in endgame and knights provide less
impact in other ways.)

I am a bit conscious that this is *mostly* a buff to a popular ancestor of
an already strong god, but it felt fun to me and I expect this should have
no more than a modest effect in the part of the game where Hep's power is
strongest.

In any case, let's see how other people find it.

(Bolster spell icon made by regret-index, combining ontoclasm's resistance
and might potion and status icons with CanofWorm's mutation resistance
icons.)

Allow monsters bound in place to attack other nearby targets

If a monster was Bound and attempted to move towards its primary target, it
would pass its turn instead. This meant that monsters bound in place next
to player allies would often just sit there and let themselves be hit
repeatedly until the status wore off.

Now they will attempt to melee some valid enemy in range, rather than pass
the turn.

(Getting spellcasters to consider casting a *spell* at some target in range
is a wildly more difficult task, but this should still be a good status
quo improvement in most situations.)

Replace Battlemage ancestors with Elementalists

While Hep battlemages may not have been 'weak' by some objective measure,
they suffered from a couple of issues:

Firstly, a recurring player complaint was that, despite being a ranged
attacker, they would repeatedly fling themselves headlong into enemies and
die rather than cast spells. However, any AI adjustments to keep them at
range would have been awkward given that their melee damage was (by design)
also considerable.

Secondly (and more subtly), despite having very different spells from
Hexer, the sort of character who would want to choose battlemage and hexer
were often nearly the same (and hexer has widely come to be seen as the
superior of the two choices - in both power and popularity).

So this is an attempt to further differentiate them by reimagining
battlemage as a more fragile ranged attacker - one whose damage output is
more consistent, but requires more effort from the player character to
protect them.

Elementalists have reduced HP compared to other ancestors, and their melee
attack is very poor, but they are cautious (and thus less likely to
approach enemies) and have higher cast rates of longer-range spells.
They have the following spells and abilities:

XL 1: Shock, Stone Arrow, Deflect Missiles
XL 14: Shock->Iceblast, Stone Arrow->Bolt of Magma
XL 19: LRD (as a 3rd attack spell), plus globally increased spell damage
XL 24: Iceblast->Permafrost Eruption, Bolt of Magma->Plasma Beam

(It's worth noting that while Deflect Missiles is helpful to the ancestor,
it's also somewhat unhelpful to the *player* as this means that the
ancestor generally cannot defend you from ranged attacks at all - they'll
just pass through and hit you instead - reinforcing their role as a pure
attacker).

This ancestor provides high damage output, but their fragility and lack of
melee ability means that the player character must be confident in their
ability to keep them alive. (Also, they make a lot of noise starting at
XL 15, which is a meaningful downside to be aware of.) The hope is that
this feels more distinct in playstyle from Hexers (giving more nuance to
the choice of ancestor type), as well as somewhat less frustrating to play
than battlemage could be. Also, they get to use some more modern and AoE
spells (battlemage's current spell list was designed when they still did
friendly fire to the player, and thus explosion spells wouldn't have been
very effective on them).
Add a couple of mages from more recent spells to randbooks.txt

Make a smaller percentage of Sif book gifts carry Sif's name

It's a little monotonous and what library worth its salt isn't filled with
tomes by *different* authors?

Weight Sif books to the player's skill a little less

With it being a little easier for Sif worshippers to utilize spells
outside their core skills, it feels appropriate to me for Sif to be a
little more diverse in their gifting at the same time.

Make Sif abilities care about Spellcasting instead of Invocations

Exegesis is one of Sif's most unique and flashy benefits, but even with it
being a bit more flexible and affordable, the investment required to get it
online can compete directly with actually being able to cast the spells
you're gifted yourself. By borrowing a page from Kiku's book, and instead
scaling off spellcasting skill, it is easier to both improve as a mage
*and* have access to Sif's neat tricks at the same time. Additionally,
encouraging the player to prioritize spellcasting (rather than school
skills) more than they otherwise would means they're also in a better
position to make use of more of Sif's varied gifts.

The skill investment required to get Exegesis online is raised a little in
compensation (and the scaling of Channel Energy reduced slightly), but it
should nonetheless be accessible earlier without sacrificing magic skills.
I expect this to be a buff to the god, but Sif has been somewhat
underperforming for a while and I also think this potentially makes Sif
gameplay more *interesting* while being very flavorful.
